steps.title: Find undergraduate students from the graduate-to-undergraduate relationship
steps.content:
Graduate students represent 35% of undergraduate students. With 4,865 graduate students surveyed:
$$4,865 = 0.35 \times U$$
Divide both sides by 0.35:
$$U = \frac{4,865}{0.35} = 13,900$$

steps.title: Find postdoctoral students from the undergraduate-to-postdoctoral relationship
steps.content:
Undergraduate students represent 6950% of postdoctoral students. Converting 6950% to decimal form: 6950% = 69.5. Using U = 13,900:
$$13,900 = 69.5 \times P$$
Divide both sides by 69.5:
$$P = \frac{13,900}{69.5} = 200$$

steps.title: Add the two student populations
steps.content:
$$13,900 + 200 = 14,100$$
The sum of undergraduate and postdoctoral students surveyed is 14,100.
